title: buildah push oci fall back to vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list contents not transformed
body:
**Description** Generating manifests with buildah is incompatible with quay.io's accepted format. **Steps to reproduce the issue:** 
[dan@bb-rhel8-docker ~]$ container=$(buildah from
docker.io/aarch64/ubuntu:latest)

[dan@bb-rhel8-docker ~]$ container2=$(buildah from
docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest)

[dan@bb-rhel8-docker ~]$ t=$(mktemp)

[dan@bb-rhel8-docker ~]$ buildah manifest create uboth
feedd4d075460e4f7785a173cfc5ff179b8bb6496e9b5794189c893286e28576

[dan@bb-rhel8-docker ~]$ buildah commit --rm --iidfile $t --manifest
uboth $container

[dan@bb-rhel8-docker ~]$ buildah --version
buildah version 1.21.4 (image-spec 1.0.1-dev, runtime-spec 1.0.2-dev)
**Describe the results you received:** Failed to be accepted by quay.io ``` Uploading manifest list failed, attempted the following formats: application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json(Error uploading manifest uboth_test to quay.io/mariadb-foundation/mariadb-devel: manifest invalid: manifest invalid), application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json(Error uploading manifest uboth_test to quay.io/mariadb-foundation/mariadb-devel: manifest invalid: manifest invalid) ``` The quay.io support request I put in resulted in the support engineer following the same steps on an older buildah version successfully pushing the manifest and the resultant inspection. ``` # skopeo inspect --raw docker://quay.io/..../ubuntu-test:both | jq '.' { "schemaVersion": 2, "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json", "manifests": [ { "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json", "size": 1228, "digest": "sha256:9c14e829fdce6780b8149a45dbcb6b78be17dc7f2291b00ef8ef08f5557ac528", "platform": { "architecture": "arm64", "os": "linux" } }, { "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json", "size": 587, "digest": "sha256:b21f3ceed9a67f388e86ecacdb8a620814153246b6818849c42ab6bbbea52284", "platform": { "architecture": "amd64", "os": "linux" } } ] } # buildah version Version: 1.19.6 Go Version: go1.16 Image Spec: 1.0.1 Runtime Spec: 1.0.2-dev CNI Spec: 0.4.0 libcni Version: image Version: 5.10.3 Git Commit: Built: Thu Jan 1 01:00:00 1970 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 ``` **Describe the results you expected:** Generated manifest list compatible with quay.io. **Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:** ``` buildah-1.21.4-1.module+el8.4.0+11989+6676f7ad.x86_64 and buildah-1.23.0-1.fc34.x86_64 ``` **Output of `buildah version`:** ``` Version: 1.21.4 Go Version: go1.15.7 Image Spec: 1.0.1-dev Runtime Spec: 1.0.2-dev CNI Spec: 0.4.0 libcni Version: v0.8.1 image Version: 5.12.0 Git Commit: Built: Tue Jul 27 09:29:47 2021 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 ``` Also tested on: ``` $ buildah version Version: 1.23.0 Go Version: go1.16.6 Image Spec: 1.0.1-dev Runtime Spec: 1.0.2-dev CNI Spec: 0.4.0 libcni Version: v0.8.1 image Version: 5.16.0 Git Commit: Built: Wed Sep 15 00:56:43 2021 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 BuildPlatform: linux/amd64 ``` https://github.com/distribution/distribution/blob/main/docs/spec/manifest-v2-2.md for manifests.mediaType list "generally be" and "could be" for media types that are implemented as "must" by quay.io it seems. or should by https://github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/blob/main/image-index.md the top level reserved mediaType be application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json (because buildah code doesn't use MediaTypeImageIndex)? Thanks for your help.

comments.body: It says "it could". But it doesn't mention `application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json` at all.

(looking at this from a c/image perspective, without trying things myself or understanding `buildah manifest` much)

So the source image is consistent (OCI index + OCI images); but the _component_ image uploads are rejected (first image is rejected as OCI, uploaded as schema2; the second image is rejected as _both_ OCI and schema2, uploaded as schema1). Then Quay rejects an OCI index, and a schema2 list that points at a (schema2, schema1) image set; it’s strict but not obviously unreasonable.

So I’m wondering why the individual uploads fail. Is it possible to create exactly the same images outside of a manifest list, and successfully upload them to Quay.io? Is it possible using the OCI format (or schema2), without an automatic conversion?

@ibazulic indicated that OCI format support is coming in the next quay.io rollout. Its already there on Red Hat quay.io.

A buildah usage that pushes images via docker repository protocol without a tag would be useful to push individual arches, and then just push the manifest list when complete.

quay.io has deployed the OCI format acceptance (or some change on their side at least) and now [(some) pushes are accepted](https://buildbot.mariadb.org/#/builders/311/builds/1217/steps/2/logs/stdio) (lines 3105 and 4638).

> A buildah usage that pushes images via docker repository protocol without a tag would be useful to push individual arches, and then just push the manifest list when complete.

That’s what the multi-arch push (with logs above), does. Do you want that split into separate commands? If so, what would that be useful for?

Yes, it would split them into separate commands. 

1.  Its mainly as a space saver (perhaps excessively lazily on my part) working around that a) a failed architecture build won't meet the build pipeline triggering required to reach the architecture count which is the criteria to push the manifest (and cleanup is fiddly).

2. Other CI frameworks (or GitHub builders) may want to push their local native arch image from the native builder (I'm lucky to have qemu working ok), and consolidate a manifest from those (fairly sure a not present image adding to a manifest isn't there currently) in a separate job once complete, and push this separately.

You really only want to push a manifest once complete, as its tag is read by every arch fetching it, so all arches should be there.
